install.stdout:
You can invoke the tool using the following command: nugraph
Tool 'nugraph' (version '0.2.0') was successfully installed.

opencli.stdout:
DESCRIPTION:
Generates dependency graphs for .NET projects and NuGet packages
USAGE:
    nugraph.dll [SOURCE] [OPTIONS]
EXAMPLES:
    nugraph.dll spectre.console/src/Spectre.Console.Cli/Spectre.Console.Cli.csproj --include-version
    nugraph.dll Serilog.Sinks.MSSqlServer --ignore Microsoft.Data.SqlClient
    nugraph.dll Newtonsoft.Json/12.0.3 --framework netstandard1.0
    nugraph.dll Azure.Core --direction TopToBottom --output Azure.Core.gv
    nugraph.dll Polly --format dot --title ""
    nugraph.dll spectre.console/src/Spectre.Console.Cli/Spectre.Console.Cli.csproj --include-version
    nugraph.dll Serilog.Sinks.MSSqlServer --ignore Microsoft.Data.SqlClient
    nugraph.dll Newtonsoft.Json/12.0.3 --framework netstandard1.0
    nugraph.dll Azure.Core --direction TopToBottom --output Azure.Core.gv
    nugraph.dll Polly --format dot --title ""
ARGUMENTS:
    [SOURCE]    The source of the graph. Can be either a directory containing a .NET project, a .NET project file (csproj) or the name of a NuGet package, optionally with a specific version, e.g. Newtonsoft.Json/13.0.3
OPTIONS:
                                          DEFAULT                                                                                                                                                                                                          
    -h, --help                                                            Prints help information                                                                                                                                                          
    -v, --version                                                         Prints version information                                                                                                                                                       
    -o, --output <OUTPUT>                                                 The path to the dependency graph output file. If not specified, the dependency graph URL is written on the standard output and an online service is opened in the default browser
    -f, --framework <FRAMEWORK>                                           The target framework to consider when building the dependency graph                                                                                                              
    -r, --runtime <RUNTIME_IDENTIFIER>                                    The target runtime to consider when building the dependency graph                                                                                                                
    -m, --format <FORMAT>                 mermaid                         The format to use when the --output option is not specified.                                                                                                                     
                                                                          Use mmd or mermaid for Mermaid Live Editor https://mermaid.live                                                                                                                  
                                                                          Use dot, gv or graphviz for Edotor https://edotor.net                                                                                                                            
    -d, --direction <GRAPH_DIRECTION>     LeftToRight                     The direction of the dependency graph. Possible values are LeftToRight and TopToBottom                                                                                           
    -t, --title <GRAPH_TITLE>             Dependency graph of [SOURCE]    The title of the dependency graph                                                                                                                                                
    -s, --include-version                                                 Include package versions in the dependency graph. E.g. Serilog/3.1.1 instead of Serilog                                                                                          
    -i, --ignore                                                          Packages to ignore in the dependency graph. May be used multiple times                                                                                                           
        --no-links                                                        Remove clickable links from the the dependency graph. Can be useful to reduce the size of the graph if you get "Maximum text size in diagram exceeded" in Mermaid Live Editor    
        --no-browser                                                      Do not open the default browser, only print the graph URL on the console when the --output option is not specified                                                               
    -l, --log <LEVEL>                     Warning                         The NuGet operations log level. Possible values are Debug, Verbose, Information, Minimal, Warning and Error
